message:
feat(planner/vdbe): add cost estimation metrics, cardinality sampling, and codegen fixes

Planner (fsqlite-planner):
- Add comprehensive cost estimation metrics: CostMetricsSnapshot with
  error ratio histogram buckets and asymmetric loss function that penalizes
  underestimation more than overestimation (slow queries are worse than
  slightly suboptimal plans).
- Add DPccp feature flag for exhaustive join enumeration on small joins
  (<=6 tables), with beam search fallback above the threshold.
- Add plans-enumerated counter for join plan enumeration observability.
- Fix histogram bucket boundary comparisons in estimate_less_than_rows
  and estimate_greater_than_rows: was comparing against self.upper/lower
  (histogram bounds) instead of bucket.upper/lower (per-bucket bounds),
  causing incorrect selectivity estimates.
- Add sampling-based cardinality estimation with fallback chain:
  histogram -> NDV -> sampling -> heuristic.
- Add planner codegen support for INSERT/UPDATE placeholder parameters
  (previously only literals were handled).

VDBE codegen:
- Add thread-local extra aggregate names for custom UDF support so
  registered aggregate functions emit AggStep/AggFinal instead of PureFunc.
- Fix aggregate detection: check for aggregate columns before applying
  rowid/index seek optimizations, since aggregates like count(*) require
  full scan paths.
- Temporarily disable index-seek path due to B-tree cursor Next() not
  correctly advancing through duplicate key entries in non-unique indexes.
- Add index scan loop for non-unique indexes (multiple rows per key).
- Implement CURRENT_TIMESTAMP/CURRENT_DATE/CURRENT_TIME literal codegen
  using epoch-to-civil-date conversion.

VDBE engine:
- Handle OPFLAG_ISUPDATE (p5=8) alongside OE_REPLACE (p5=5) in Insert
  opcode to enable UPDATE operations that delete-then-reinsert.
- Fall through to MemDatabase path when a transaction-backed page is
  zero/invalid but the table exists in MemDatabase (e.g., sqlite_master).
- Use saturating_neg() for shift amount negation to prevent overflow
  panic on i64::MIN.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
